title: Severance usage cache
description: ~/.claude/severance/usage.json — signal snapshot written by the statusline bridge or the Tier-2 OAuth fetch. `severance-lib.sh` produces the `normalized` block from whichever tier fired; consumers read only `normalized`. See docs/upstream-snapshots/statusline.md.

ts.description: Unix epoch seconds when this snapshot was written.

rate_limits.description: Raw Tier-1 rate_limits object, persisted verbatim so a future shape change is recoverable. Absent for oauth/ccusage tiers.

cost.description: Cost block (statusline `cost`, or synthesized from ccusage).

window.description: A rate-limit window. Values may be null when the window is absent from the signal (statusline windows can be independently absent).

resets_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC. Normalizer converts statusline epoch seconds to this form.

extraUsage.description: Usage-credit state. Tier-2 (OAuth) only; is_enabled/used_credits are null under Tier-1/Tier-3.
